question_text: Which of the following is a characteristic of particles in a solid?

options.label: A
options.text: Particles are tightly packed in fixed positions and vibrate in place

options.label: B
options.text: Particles are spread far apart and move freely around the container

options.label: C
options.text: Particles are touching but can slide past each other easily

options.label: D
options.text: Particles have no forces between them and move at very high speeds

explanation: In solids, particles are held tightly in fixed positions by strong forces. They can only vibrate in place, which is why solids have a fixed shape and volume. The other options describe liquids or gases incorrectly.
